root_text.an4.177:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā rāhulo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ rāhulaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an4.177:2.1: “Yā ca, rāhula, ajjhattikā pathavīdhātu yā ca bāhirā pathavīdhātu, pathavīdhāturevesā.
root_text.an4.177:2.2: ‘Taṁ netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti, evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.177:2.3: Evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā pathavīdhātuyā nibbindati, pathavīdhātuyā cittaṁ virājeti.
root_text.an4.177:3.1: Yā ca, rāhula, ajjhattikā āpodhātu yā ca bāhirā āpodhātu, āpodhāturevesā.
root_text.an4.177:3.2: ‘Taṁ netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti, evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.177:3.3: Evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā āpodhātuyā nibbindati, āpodhātuyā cittaṁ virājeti.
root_text.an4.177:4.1: Yā ca, rāhula, ajjhattikā tejodhātu yā ca bāhirā tejodhātu, tejodhāturevesā.
root_text.an4.177:4.2: ‘Taṁ netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti, evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.177:4.3: Evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā tejodhātuyā nibbindati, tejodhātuyā cittaṁ virājeti.
root_text.an4.177:5.1: Yā ca, rāhula, ajjhattikā vāyodhātu yā ca bāhirā vāyodhātu, vāyodhāturevesā.
root_text.an4.177:5.2: ‘Taṁ netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti, evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.177:5.3: Evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā vāyodhātuyā nibbindati, vāyodhātuyā cittaṁ virājeti.
root_text.an4.177:6.1: Yato kho, rāhula, bhikkhu imāsu catūsu dhātūsu nevattānaṁ na attaniyaṁ samanupassati, ayaṁ vuccati, rāhula, bhikkhu acchecchi taṇhaṁ, vivattayi saṁyojanaṁ, sammā mānābhisamayā antamakāsi dukkhassā”ti.

translation_text.an4.177:1.1: Then Venerable Rāhula went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an4.177:2.1: “Rāhula, the interior earth element and the exterior earth element are just the earth element.
translation_text.an4.177:2.2: This should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.an4.177:2.3: When you truly see with right understanding, you grow disillusioned with the earth element, detaching the mind from the earth element.
translation_text.an4.177:3.1: The interior water element and the exterior water element are just the water element.
translation_text.an4.177:3.2: This should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.an4.177:3.3: When you truly see with right understanding, you grow disillusioned with the water element, detaching the mind from the water element.
translation_text.an4.177:4.1: The interior fire element and the exterior fire element are just the fire element.
translation_text.an4.177:4.2: This should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.an4.177:4.3: When you truly see with right understanding, you grow disillusioned with the fire element, detaching the mind from the fire element.
translation_text.an4.177:5.1: The interior air element and the exterior air element are just the air element.
translation_text.an4.177:5.2: This should be truly seen with right understanding like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.an4.177:5.3: When you truly see with right understanding, you grow disillusioned with the air element, detaching the mind from the air element.
translation_text.an4.177:6.1: When a mendicant sees these four elements as neither self nor belonging to self, they’re called a mendicant who has cut off craving, cast off the fetters, and by rightly comprehending conceit has made an end of suffering.”
